test_output_summary: 202 tests passed (32 new in test_prompts.py + 170 existing), 0 failures. All lint checks pass (ruff clean).
implementation_notes: Created the jarvis.prompts package with SUPERVISOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT constant following the system-prompt-template-specialist patterns. The prompt establishes Jarvis's identity as a general-purpose attended conversation agent, states the cheapest-that-fits/escalate-on-need model-selection philosophy, and includes {date} and {domain_prompt} runtime placeholders per the template convention. Scope invariant enforced: no mention of call_specialist, queue_build, morning-briefing, task (as tool), or subagent names. Enhanced tests/conftest.py with four shared fixtures: fake_llm (FakeListChatModel), test_config (JarvisConfig with fake endpoint), in_memory_store (LangGraph InMemoryStore), and app_state (placeholder dict). All fixtures use lazy imports to avoid import-time side effects.
requirements_addressed: AC-003: fake_llm fixture callable with canned responses
AC-005: No forbidden tool/subagent names in supervisor prompt

completion_promises.criterion_text: `from jarvis.prompts.supervisor_prompt import SUPERVISOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT` works; the constant is non-empty.
completion_promises.evidence: Created src/jarvis/prompts/supervisor_prompt.py with SUPERVISOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT as a typed str constant. Created src/jarvis/prompts/__init__.py re-export shim with __all__. 11 tests verify import from both module and package paths, non-empty content, str type, {date}/{domain_prompt} placeholders, format resolution, Jarvis identity, attended-conversation posture, and cheapest-that-fits philosophy.

completion_promises.criterion_text: `pytest tests/` collects successfully (no tests defined yet in this task, but collection works).
completion_promises.evidence: pytest tests/ --collect-only reports 202 tests collected successfully. TestAC002PytestCollection class with 2 tests explicitly validates collection. All test files have proper __init__.py and conftest.py structure.

completion_promises.criterion_text: `fake_llm` fixture is callable in a test and returns canned responses without hitting a real provider.
completion_promises.evidence: Added fake_llm fixture to tests/conftest.py using langchain_core FakeListChatModel with 3 canned responses. TestAC003FakeLlm class with 4 tests verifies: fixture not None, first invoke returns 'Canned response 1', second invoke returns 'Canned response 2', and instance is a BaseChatModel. No network calls made.

Added test_config fixture to tests/conftest.py that creates JarvisConfig with openai_base_url='http://fake-endpoint/v1' in a clean environment (patched os.environ) and calls validate_provider_keys() to ensure no ConfigurationError. TestAC004TestConfigFixture class with 7 tests verifies: not None, is JarvisConfig instance, has fake URL, correct defaults (log_level=INFO, supervisor_model=openai:jarvis-reasoner, memory_store_backend=in_memory), and validate_provider_keys() succeeds on re-call.

completion_promises.criterion_text: No mention of `call_specialist`, `queue_build`, `morning-briefing`, `task`, or subagent names in the supervisor prompt (scope invariant).
completion_promises.evidence: SUPERVISOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT contains no forbidden terms. TestAC005ScopeInvariant class with 3 tests verifies: no forbidden terms (call_specialist, queue_build, morning-briefing, morning_briefing, talk_prep), no task-as-tool references (task( or 'task' patterns), and no subagent names (jarvis-reasoner, implementer, evaluator, builder). The prompt module docstring documents the scope invariant explicitly.
